posts.text:
The true cost of war. 6 more Americans have been killed while executing their mission during Operation Epic Fury, never to return home to their loved ones.

This loss of life is devastating, and I’m sending my most sincere condolences to their families who pay this tremendous sacrifice to our nation, too.

My thoughts are with the Air Force and the entire military community in the wake of this tragedy. We know they will always answer the call and execute their missions, despite their grief.

Con

Do you consider the more than 23,000 Muslim constituents living in your state right now to be "enemies" too?

What you call being “politically correct” is just being a decent human being.

And not fueling Islamophobic hate and violence against other people.


Coach Tommy Tuberville (@SenTuberville)

I don’t give a rip about being politically correct. 

Innocent Americans are being gunned down in the streets almost daily by Radical Islamists whose ‘religion’ teaches them it’s righteous to kill Ch

Horrified by the attack on Temple Israel in Michigan.

It is unacceptable that another Jewish community is left traumatized by such blatant antisemitism in America.

Everyone should be able to pray in their house of worship without fear.

War is not a f*cking video game.
War is not a f*cking video game.
War is not a f*cking video game.

Seven Americans are dead.


The White House (@WhiteHouse)

UNDEFEATED.

Video
  





— https://nitter.net/WhiteHouse/status/2032115039985881556#m

Shrapnel wounds. Brain injuries. Burns.

Trump is throwing our troops into unjustified foreign wars while gutting our VA and making it harder to get the care they earned when they come home.

Video

Absolutely infuriating.

The Iranian missile that Hegseth downplayed as a "squirter getting through" killed six Americans and left dozens with traumatic brain injuries, burns and shrapnel in their bodies.

The true cost of war is so much more than a billion dollars a day. It's paid for in the blood and sacrifices of our troops—who still don't know what the mission is, when it will end or whether their wounds are justified.

We need to put this Administration under oath in a public hearing. Immed

This president never served in uniform—but has no problem using our troops as cannon fodder in a war he cannot justify.

Enough. We need to put them under oath to get the answers our troops deserve.

Video

Before throwing a new generation of troops into war, they made sure to:

Torpedo our VA.
Enact the biggest health care cut in history.
Shutter hospitals.

Don’t you dare tell me you care about our heroes when you're doing everything you can to undermine care when they get home.


ABC News (@ABC)

As many as 724,000 service members, their families and veterans may rely on health care at hospitals that face financial vulnerability, partly due to cuts in President Trump's megabill, according to a n

They're hiding their incompetence behind the valor of our men and women in uniform.

As if to question why we're at war is to question our heroes. No, it's to make sure the mission is worth their sacrifices.

Our troops will always do their job.
Republicans need to do theirs.

Video

As many as 140 servicemembers injured. 7 killed. Thousands of Americans stranded in the region with a target on their backs and no escape.

All for a war that a draft dodger cannot even articulate a coherent and consistent justification for.

How. Is. This. America. First.

Trump burned $5.6 billion on munitions alone in the first two days of his unjustified, unauthorized war.

But they don't have money for your health care.

As if food prices weren’t hitting your pocketbook hard enough, now gas prices are another sucker punch.

This is Trump’s doing. Higher costs and more war is not what he promised.


The New York Times (@nytimes)

Breaking News: Oil prices surged above $100 a barrel for the first time in almost four years amid growing concerns about supplies as the war in the Middle East continues. nyti.ms/40RlVy6




— https://nitter.net/nytimes/status/2030776562144981266#m

Extending my heartfelt condolences to the loved ones of Sgt. Benjamin N. Pennington, the seventh American servicemember killed in Trump’s war of choice.

Our troops will always answer the call. Their leaders owe it to them to ensure their sacrifices are justified.

We shall not see the likes of the great Reverend Jesse Jackson again. He leaves us with a legacy our state will never forget.

Rest in my power, my friend.

War is not a f*cking video game.

Six Americans are dead and thousands more are at needless risk because of your illegal, unjustified war.

And you're calling this a flawless victory.


The White House (@WhiteHouse)

JUSTICE THE AMERICAN WAY. 🇺🇸🔥

Video
  





— https://nitter.net/WhiteHouse/status/2029741548791853331#m

Your costs are skyrocketing, jobs are cratering and Trump is starting the wars he promised he'd stop.

It's like he is trying to break all his promises.


CNN (@CNN)

The US economy lost 92,000 jobs last month, far worse than expected.
cnn.it/4ba3IRh





— https://nitter.net/CNN/status/2029915418370908424#m

Thousands of Americans are stranded in the Middle East receiving conflicting guidance while their President admits there was no evacuation plan.

That is unacceptable. Secretary Rubio must ramp up crisis efforts to get Americans to safety immediately.

Should've been fired a long, long time ago.

Next, the Trump Administration needs to work with Congress to reform DHS and prevent more Americans from being executed by their own government. The bare minimum.


CNN Breaking News (@cnnbrk)

President Trump ousts Kristi Noem as Homeland Security chief and names Oklahoma Sen. Markwayne Mullin as her replacement.
cnn.it/4b76kPY




— https://nitter.net/cnnbrk/status/2029632133774389465#m
